_note: TabICL v2 exported at the package DEFAULT (v2) architecture, weight-free (opset 18). H is DYNAMIC (no fixed feature bucket, no padding, no cat_mask, no d input). Input y carries ONLY the training labels: its length S IS train_size (there is no separate train_size / cat_mask / d input). Output logits[1,T,C]: rows >= S are the test predictions (rows < S carry the model's own values and are ignored). C = 10 for classification (max_classes); for regression C = 999 QUANTILE logits (not a single value) — TabICL maps these to a point estimate via its quantile head downstream. Per-task tensor_map: classification uses ../resources/tensor_map_tabicl_classification.json; regression's is ../resources/tensor_map_tabicl_regression.json (identical key set). Real-weight injection requires the checkpoint config to match the exported dims (v2 defaults); verify before onboarding. Preprocessing: TabICL standardizes X internally on train rows (z-score baked into the graph); the sklearn wrapper additionally ordinal-encodes + imputes + shuffles features — a minimal z-score+ordinal C++ preprocess is close but not identical (see FEASIBILITY).
